title: Intersight Contract and Warranty Compliance
exclusions: Assets that have never been claimed in Intersight, so they never get a `RegisteredDevice` link on the **asset.ContractInformation** object, is excluded from this data path until claim completes. Rows with `LineStatus` set to a decommissioning state the team agrees means “intentional retired” (your CSV should tag these, not the alert) is excluded from renewal queues. If the authoritative contract record still lives in SAP Ariba, ServiceNow CMDB, or a partner extranet, and that row has not been reflected into the Intersight contract feed yet, renewal evidence may exist outside Splunk—treat the gap as a data integration defect, not a green bill of health. Extended-warranty coverage that is real on paper but not visible to Cisco’s `Source` and `ServiceLevel` path Intersight ingests should be reconciled in procurement tools; UC-19.1.20 covers firmware string posture and UC-19.1.21 covers HCL stack posture, while UC-19.1.19 remains the live cond/Alarms signal—none of those replace a commercial contract window. Nutanix AOS lifecycle is UC-19.1.27 (different stack entirely).
evidence: Saved search `UC-19.1.24-Contract-Expiry-Posture` (or your standard name) on `index=cisco_intersight` using sourcetype `cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation` with a retained table export of `contract_status`, `end_date`, `days_to_expiry`, `severity_bucket`, `service_level`, and joined owner columns from `intersight_contract_thresholds.csv` / `intersight_critical_assets.csv`, versioned in your change system.
dataSources: `index=cisco_intersight` (or the governed index you bind to the Cisco Intersight modular inputs) with primary sourcetype **`cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation`**, the Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight projection of the Intersight `asset.ContractInformation` managed object from `GET /api/v1/asset/ContractInformations` (plural list path; singular class name on the model page). The templated name `cisco:intersight:contracts` is *not* a vendor sourcetype in current TA 5953 builds. Expect JSON with contract posture fields such as **ContractStatus** (for example `Active`, `Expired`, `NotCovered`, `Unknown`), **ContractNumber**, **EndDate** and **StartDate** in ISO-8601, **ServiceLevel** (for example `SNT`, `PSUP`, `PSRP`, `C4S`, `8X5XNBD` depending on offer), **ServiceLine**, **ContractDescription**, **Source** (Cisco or partner channel hints), **LineStatus**, **BillTo** (object), **Moid**, **CreateTime**/**ModTime**, and a relationship to **RegisteredDevice** that your TA may expose as a string Moid, nested keys, or `registered_device` in snake case. For hostname, serial, platform, and **ConnectionStatus**, optionally left-join the companion feed `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration` (`asset.DeviceRegistration`, `GET /api/v1/asset/DeviceRegistrations`, align with UC-19.1.33) on the registration **Moid** equal to the contract’s **RegisteredDevice** reference. For rack-friendly display name and serial when registration rows are thin, add a second left-join to `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` (`compute.PhysicalSummary`) on the same Moid your tenant uses for inventory parity—confirm with five raw events before you freeze the join in production, because object graphs differ between IMM, UCS, and HyperFlex claims.
app: Install the Splunk Add-on for Cisco Intersight on disk as `Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight` ([Splunkbase 5953](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5953)) on the Heavy Forwarder (or the node that runs the modular input) and on Search Heads for fields, scheduled searches, and lookups. Marketing labels such as “Cisco Intersight Add-on” on Splunkbase map to the same package name. The integration authenticates to Intersight with a Cisco-issued API key pair (KeyId and Secret) stored through the add-on account workflow or Splunk’s credential store—never a cleartext secret in Git-tracked `local/`.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.1.24 — Intersight Contract and Warranty compliance: asset.ContractInformation via cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation; EndDate strptime with ISO fallbacks; days_to_expiry; tiered severity_bucket; optional joins to asset.DeviceRegistration and compute.PhysicalSummary; governance lookups.")`
index=cisco_intersight (sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation" OR sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetcontractinfo") earliest=-7d@d
| eval contract_moid=toString(coalesce(Moid, moid, mo_id, ""))
| eval end_date_raw=toString(coalesce(EndDate, end_date, ""))
| eval end_epoch=coalesce(
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), null()),
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), null()),
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), null()),
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"), null())
  )
| eval days_to_expiry=if(isnotnull(end_epoch), round((end_epoch-now())/86400, 0), null())
| eval contract_status=toString(coalesce(ContractStatus, contract_status, ""))
| eval contract_number=toString(coalesce(ContractNumber, contract_number, ""))
| eval service_level=toString(coalesce(ServiceLevel, service_level, ""))
| eval line_status=toString(coalesce(LineStatus, line_status, ""))
| eval source_sys=toString(coalesce(Source, source, source_system, ""))
| eval csl=lower(contract_status)
| eval lsl=lower(line_status)
| where lsl=="" OR lsl!="inactive"
| eval regdev_moid=trim(replace(replace(toString(coalesce(RegisteredDevice, registered_device, "")),"[\\[\\]\\\"]",""),"Moid:",""))
| join type=left regdev_moid [
  search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration" earliest=-7d@d
  | eval regdev_moid=toString(coalesce(Moid, moid, ""))
  | eval j_hostname=toString(coalesce(DeviceHostname, device_hostname, ""))
  | eval j_serial=toString(coalesce(Serial, serial, ""))
  | eval j_platform=toString(coalesce(PlatformType, platform_type, ""))
  | eval j_conn=toString(coalesce(ConnectionStatus, connection_status, ""))
  | where isnotnull(regdev_moid) AND regdev_moid!=""
  | stats latest(j_hostname) as dr_hostname, latest(j_serial) as dr_serial, latest(j_platform) as dr_platform, latest(j_conn) as dr_connection by regdev_moid
  ]
| join type=left regdev_moid [
  search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-7d@d
  | eval regdev_moid=toString(coalesce(Moid, moid, ""))
  | eval cps_name=toString(coalesce(Name, name, ""))
  | eval cps_serial=toString(coalesce(Serial, serial, ""))
  | where isnotnull(regdev_moid) AND regdev_moid!=""
  | stats latest(cps_name) as cps_name, latest(cps_serial) as cps_serial by regdev_moid
  ]
| eval serial=toString(coalesce(cps_serial, dr_serial, ""))
| eval asset_name=toString(coalesce(cps_name, dr_hostname, "unresolved-"+contract_moid))
| eval end_date=if(isnotnull(end_epoch), strftime(end_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), end_date_raw)
| eval service_tier=case(
    match(upper(service_level),"^(PSUP|C4S|L12|PRP)$"), "production_premium",
    match(upper(service_level),"^(SNT|SNTR|SNTC|8X5|NBD|8X5XNBD|L1S)$"), "standard_smb",
    match(upper(service_level),"LIC|LICNS"), "software_line",
    1=1, "other")
| lookup intersight_contract_thresholds service_tier OUTPUT warn_days, high_days, critical_days, business_owner, action_owner, change_ticket_required
| fillnull value=180 warn_days
| fillnull value=60 high_days
| fillnull value=30 critical_days
| lookup intersight_critical_assets serial_or_moid AS serial OUTPUT asset_role, business_service, sla_tier, named_owner
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    csl=="expired" OR csl=="notcovered" OR csl=="unknown" OR (isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<0), "expired",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=critical_days, "critical_30d",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=high_days, "high_60d",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=warn_days, "warning_90d",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=(warn_days*2), "low_180d",
    1=1, "compliant")
| eval bo_lkp=toString(coalesce(named_owner, business_owner, ""))
| eval ao_lkp=toString(coalesce(action_owner, ""))
| eval business_owner=if(bo_lkp!="", bo_lkp, if(asset_role=="lab", "lab-coordinator@example.com", "category-owner@example.com"))
| eval action_owner=if(ao_lkp!="", ao_lkp, if(asset_role=="lab", "lab-coordinator@example.com", "procurement-vendor-management@example.com"))
| where severity_bucket IN ("expired","critical_30d","high_60d","warning_90d","low_180d")
| eval sev_n=case(
    severity_bucket=="expired", 1000,
    severity_bucket=="critical_30d", 900,
    severity_bucket=="high_60d", 800,
    severity_bucket=="warning_90d", 700,
    severity_bucket=="low_180d", 600,
    1=1, 0)
| table asset_name, serial, contract_status, contract_number, service_level, end_date, days_to_expiry, severity_bucket, business_owner, action_owner
| sort 0 - sev_n days_to_expiry
description: Watches the `asset.ContractInformation` object as Splunk ingests it on `cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation` from `Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight` (Splunkbase 5953) against `GET /api/v1/asset/ContractInformations`, coalesces camelCase and snake_case field spellings, parses **EndDate** with stacked `strptime` fallbacks, derives `days_to_expiry` against `now()`, and buckets `severity_bucket` into at least six runbook tiers (expired, critical_30d, high_60d, warning_90d, low_180d, and compliant) before filtering to the non-compliant buckets. The pipeline optionally left-joins `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration` and `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` on the `RegisteredDevice` Moid to attach hostname, serial, and platform context, then layers `intersight_contract_thresholds.csv` for tier-specific day-bands and `intersight_critical_assets.csv` to weight named owners. This is explicitly the **commercial** entitlement and renewal posture axis—distinct from **cond/Alarms** in UC-19.1.19, firmware `RunningFirmware` equality in UC-19.1.20, and **cond.HclStatus** in UC-19.1.21.
value: A dropped contract shows up in leadership conversations as a surprise capital ask, a missed renewal window, or a failed audit, not a pink fault LED. A coverage gap is days-long wait on advance replacement during a sev-1, missing software entitlement and TAC priority when a board-level software defect lands, a procurement team blind-sided at quarter close when auto-renewal slipped past governance, and an awkward board answer when an auditor asks for proof of premium support on systems that back customer-facing **Service Levels** you already promised. Keeping contract posture beside—but not conflated with—firmware, HCL, and alarm streams lets vendor management, compute platform owners, and security attestation work from one Intersight-backed evidence trail instead of a spreadsheet no one opened until something broke.
implementation: Install `Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight`, add a six-hour (or daily) poll for `asset.ContractInformation` with endpoint `/api/v1/asset/ContractInformations`, keep `asset.DeviceRegistration` and `compute.PhysicalSummary` on for join coverage, place `intersight_contract_thresholds.csv` and `intersight_critical_assets.csv` in a governed app, grant the `intersight_contract_reader` role, save the `spl` as a weekly report and a non-paging alert that files CSV to your ticketing and monthly PDF to leadership.
visualization: Row of single values: count of `severity_bucket=expired`, count of `critical_30d`, and distinct `asset_name` with `change_ticket_required=true` from the threshold lookup. Primary sortable `table` of the columns in the `spl` with colour ranges on `days_to_expiry`. Heatmap: Intersight account or customer `business_service` (from the critical-assets lookup) by `severity_bucket` for portfolio review. Trend: weekly count of `expired` rows to show renewal process effectiveness.

references.title: Cisco — Product technical support and services portfolio (service-level context)
Splunk Lantern — use case and onboarding hub

knownFalsePositives: A renewal in flight on the Cisco side can leave an older row marked Expired for a day or two before the new Active record lands from Smart Net Total Care back-end sync—use your finance tickler date as a second source before you punish an owner. Rows whose Source field is `cisco_partner` often have the legally-binding renewal in the partner portal first; Splunk is reflecting Cisco’s channel timing, not your procurement lie. A lab on `8X5XNBD` beside production on `PSUP` is a lab policy choice, not a sev-1, when `intersight_critical_assets.csv` marks `asset_role=lab`. A hardware-refresh month where the decommissioned serial is Expired on purpose while the replacement serial is not yet Active in Intersight should map through `intersight_replacement_serial_map.csv` in your runbook, not a bridge page. M&A or entity-change windows where `BillTo` still shows the pre-close legal name can spook finance; route to procurement master data, not a compute incident. When Cisco renames a ServiceLevel code (`SNTC` to `SNT` style drift), a trend dashboard can briefly look worse until lookup tables catch up. Managed-service and MSP tenants with strict Intersight RBAC can show a missing contract row for operators who are only scoped to a child org—the gap can be permissions on the key, not an absent entitlement. A midnight `EndDate` in UTC over a different calendar quarter in local business time is a common procurement-versus-engineering argument—agree a single `effective_close_tz` in your runbook, not a second Splunk system.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud 9.2+ search tier with a governed index (here `cisco_intersight`) and the Heavy Forwarder (or other modular-input-capable) host allowed outbound TCP 443 to the Intersight API hostname for your account model. On disk the directory name must be exactly `Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight` from [Splunkbase 5953](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5953) on the input node and on Search Heads that author alerts.
• A Cisco Intersight API key pair with read access to the **asset.ContractInformation** and **asset.DeviceRegistration** families at minimum, plus `compute.PhysicalSummary` if you will enrich names and serials. Store credentials in the add-on account object so `passwords.conf` (or the Cloud equivalent) holds secrets, not a flat `Secret=` line in a repo.
• Governance: the fields `ContractStatus`, `ServiceLevel`, and `EndDate` are business-sensitive. Restrict indexes to a role like `intersight_contract_reader` for dashboards; limit export actions on alert hooks so CSV attachments do not go to a public channel.
• License and volume: `asset.ContractInformation` is typically low frequency per device compared with stats MOs, but JSON payloads can be large. Budget retention with evidence policies—many teams keep 13–18 months to span fiscal renewals. Time sync (NTP) on the input host matters for `now()`-based `days_to_expiry` math.
• Educational note: the templated `cisco:intersight:contracts` sourcetype is fictional. Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight materialises the managed object on **`cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation`**, derived from the **asset.ContractInformation** class via REST endpoint `/api/v1/asset/ContractInformations`. If an older design doc still lists `cisco:intersight:contracts`, update it so junior engineers do not build searches against air.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) On the Intersight input host, merge local configuration under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/`. The exact vendor stanza family name has varied between TA build lines; align keys with `default/inputs.conf` for the package you installed before you enable anything. A structural illustration the customer should reconcile line-for-line to their `default/inputs.conf` is:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/inputs.conf
# Heavy Forwarder — OAuth2 account configured in the add-on UI, not in this file as cleartext

[ciscointersight://prod_intersight_contracts]
account = intersight_readonly_5953
index = cisco_intersight
disabled = 0
interval = 21600
endpoint = /api/v1/asset/ContractInformations
# If your build nests endpoint under a base URL stanza, mirror the default file instead of this comment block.
# mo_class = asset.ContractInformation   # if your TA uses explicit class strings
# sourcetype_prefix = cisco:intersight  # if your build requires an explicit prefix
```

(2) Add parallel inputs (or a combined MO list, depending on TA version) for `asset.DeviceRegistration` and `compute.PhysicalSummary` if they are not already enabled—the joins in the saved search are optional but strongly recommended. Registration powers hostname and connector posture context (see UC-19.1.33); **PhysicalSummary** provides display `Name` and `Serial` when registration rows are sparse. Poll intervals for inventory-style classes of 5–15 minutes are common; contract information can follow the six-hour cadence above if your vendor-management process is weekly.

(3) `props.conf` emphasises JSON discipline for the contract sourcetype:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/props.conf
[cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation]
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
KV_MODE = none
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 0
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
# Prefer the MO ModTime for evidence ordering when present; fall back to event receipt time
description = Intersight asset.ContractInformation feed for commercial entitlement posture
# TIME_PREFIX / TIME_FORMAT: align to your TA; many builds map ModTime with fractional seconds
```

(4) In a small wrapper app you own, for example `uc19_intersight_contracts_governance`, add `lookups/*.csv` and `transforms.conf` for the two governance tables:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/uc19_intersight_contracts_governance/local/transforms.conf
[intersight_contract_thresholds]
filename = intersight_contract_thresholds.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0

[intersight_critical_assets]
filename = intersight_critical_assets.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0

[intersight_replacement_serial_map]
filename = intersight_replacement_serial_map.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
```

(5) `intersight_contract_thresholds.csv` (headers must match the saved search) — at least six example rows, production and lab on different day-bands. Notice how **warn_days** is wide for `production_premium` and tight for `lab`:

```csv
service_tier,warn_days,high_days,critical_days,business_owner,action_owner,change_ticket_required
production_premium,180,60,30,category-owner@example.com,procurement-vendor@example.com,1
standard_smb,150,50,30,category-owner@example.com,procurement-tac@example.com,1
software_line,90,40,20,stakeholder-software@example.com,software-vendor@example.com,0
tier2_datacenter,150,50,30,regional-dc@example.com,regional-proc@example.com,1
tier1_edge,90,30,20,edge-sre@example.com,edge-proc@example.com,1
lab,60,20,7,lab-coordinator@example.com,lab-coordinator@example.com,0
other,150,50,30,category-owner@example.com,procurement-vendor@example.com,0
poc,45,20,5,poc-sponsor@example.com,poc-sponsor@example.com,0
```

(6) `intersight_critical_assets.csv` — six+ examples tying serial (or, if you must, MOID strings) to business language:

```csv
serial_or_moid,asset_role,business_service,sla_tier,named_owner
FCH1234A12B,production,AMER-Retail-Commerce,gold,owner-retail@example.com
FCH1234A12C,production,AMER-Retail-Commerce,gold,owner-retail@example.com
FCH2244B99Z,lab,LAB-Perf,gold,lab-coordinator@example.com
FCHABCD0001,production,EMEA-ERP-SAP,gold,owner-erp@example.com
FCHABCD0002,production,EMEA-ERP-SAP,gold,owner-erp@example.com
FCHWXYZ4242,production,APAC-Analytics,silver,owner-ml@example.com
Moid-0xdeadbeef,production,GLOBAL-DW,gold,owner-dw@example.com
```

(7) Optional but referenced in the false-positive narrative, `intersight_replacement_serial_map.csv` to bridge refresh windows:

```csv
old_serial,new_serial,note
FCHOLD1111,FCHNEW2222,2026Q2 cage swap
cFCHAA1234,CFCHNE5678,RMA for disk path
FCHHXP001,FCHHXP901,head-node replacement
FCHCOLD01,FCHCNEW02,C-series reseat project
FCHCOLD03,FCHCNEW04,secondary rack move
FCHOLD555,FCH777NEW,demo-to-prod transfer
FCHPREFAIL,FCHPREFIX,spare pre-provisioned
FCHBETA01,FCHBETANEW,beta line cleanup
FCHBETA02,FCHBETANEW,beta line cleanup two
FCHBETA03,FCHBETANEW,beta line cleanup three
```

(8) Role wiring in `authorize.conf` (conceptual): create `intersight_contract_reader` with `srchIndexesAllowed` including `cisco_intersight` and `srchFilter` to strip unrelated sourcetypes if you must segment duties. Map dashboard and report permissions to that role, not to `admin`.

(9) **Validation handshake before you trust joins:** run `| stats count by sourcetype` in the last day, `fieldsummary` on five random **assetcontractinformation** events, and a manual compare of `RegisteredDevice` and `Moid` strings against a `/api/v1/asset/ContractInformations?$top=1&$select=*` call with the same read-only key. If `RegisteredDevice` is embedded JSON rather than a flat Moid, extend the `coalesce()` block with the dotted path you see, not the guess in this text.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Authoritative SPL string—must match the `spl` field in this use case and the first-line `comment` macro. If you do not have a `comment` macro, define a no-op in `macros.conf` or remove the backtick line only in non-production scratch searches, not in the saved object you rely on for evidence.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.1.24 — Intersight Contract and Warranty compliance: asset.ContractInformation via cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation; EndDate strptime with ISO fallbacks; days_to_expiry; tiered severity_bucket; optional joins to asset.DeviceRegistration and compute.PhysicalSummary; governance lookups.")`
index=cisco_intersight (sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation" OR sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetcontractinfo") earliest=-7d@d
| eval contract_moid=toString(coalesce(Moid, moid, mo_id, ""))
| eval end_date_raw=toString(coalesce(EndDate, end_date, ""))
| eval end_epoch=coalesce(
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), null()),
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), null()),
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), null()),
    if(end_date_raw!="" AND isnotnull(strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N")), strptime(end_date_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"), null())
  )
| eval days_to_expiry=if(isnotnull(end_epoch), round((end_epoch-now())/86400, 0), null())
| eval contract_status=toString(coalesce(ContractStatus, contract_status, ""))
| eval contract_number=toString(coalesce(ContractNumber, contract_number, ""))
| eval service_level=toString(coalesce(ServiceLevel, service_level, ""))
| eval line_status=toString(coalesce(LineStatus, line_status, ""))
| eval source_sys=toString(coalesce(Source, source, source_system, ""))
| eval csl=lower(contract_status)
| eval lsl=lower(line_status)
| where lsl=="" OR lsl!="inactive"
| eval regdev_moid=trim(replace(replace(toString(coalesce(RegisteredDevice, registered_device, "")),"[\\[\\]\\\"]",""),"Moid:",""))
| join type=left regdev_moid [
  search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration" earliest=-7d@d
  | eval regdev_moid=toString(coalesce(Moid, moid, ""))
  | eval j_hostname=toString(coalesce(DeviceHostname, device_hostname, ""))
  | eval j_serial=toString(coalesce(Serial, serial, ""))
  | eval j_platform=toString(coalesce(PlatformType, platform_type, ""))
  | eval j_conn=toString(coalesce(ConnectionStatus, connection_status, ""))
  | where isnotnull(regdev_moid) AND regdev_moid!=""
  | stats latest(j_hostname) as dr_hostname, latest(j_serial) as dr_serial, latest(j_platform) as dr_platform, latest(j_conn) as dr_connection by regdev_moid
  ]
| join type=left regdev_moid [
  search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-7d@d
  | eval regdev_moid=toString(coalesce(Moid, moid, ""))
  | eval cps_name=toString(coalesce(Name, name, ""))
  | eval cps_serial=toString(coalesce(Serial, serial, ""))
  | where isnotnull(regdev_moid) AND regdev_moid!=""
  | stats latest(cps_name) as cps_name, latest(cps_serial) as cps_serial by regdev_moid
  ]
| eval serial=toString(coalesce(cps_serial, dr_serial, ""))
| eval asset_name=toString(coalesce(cps_name, dr_hostname, "unresolved-"+contract_moid))
| eval end_date=if(isnotnull(end_epoch), strftime(end_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), end_date_raw)
| eval service_tier=case(
    match(upper(service_level),"^(PSUP|C4S|L12|PRP)$"), "production_premium",
    match(upper(service_level),"^(SNT|SNTR|SNTC|8X5|NBD|8X5XNBD|L1S)$"), "standard_smb",
    match(upper(service_level),"LIC|LICNS"), "software_line",
    1=1, "other")
| lookup intersight_contract_thresholds service_tier OUTPUT warn_days, high_days, critical_days, business_owner, action_owner, change_ticket_required
| fillnull value=180 warn_days
| fillnull value=60 high_days
| fillnull value=30 critical_days
| lookup intersight_critical_assets serial_or_moid AS serial OUTPUT asset_role, business_service, sla_tier, named_owner
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    csl=="expired" OR csl=="notcovered" OR csl=="unknown" OR (isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<0), "expired",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=critical_days, "critical_30d",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=high_days, "high_60d",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=warn_days, "warning_90d",
    isnotnull(days_to_expiry) AND days_to_expiry<=(warn_days*2), "low_180d",
    1=1, "compliant")
| eval bo_lkp=toString(coalesce(named_owner, business_owner, ""))
| eval ao_lkp=toString(coalesce(action_owner, ""))
| eval business_owner=if(bo_lkp!="", bo_lkp, if(asset_role=="lab", "lab-coordinator@example.com", "category-owner@example.com"))
| eval action_owner=if(ao_lkp!="", ao_lkp, if(asset_role=="lab", "lab-coordinator@example.com", "procurement-vendor-management@example.com"))
| where severity_bucket IN ("expired","critical_30d","high_60d","warning_90d","low_180d")
| eval sev_n=case(
    severity_bucket=="expired", 1000,
    severity_bucket=="critical_30d", 900,
    severity_bucket=="high_60d", 800,
    severity_bucket=="warning_90d", 700,
    severity_bucket=="low_180d", 600,
    1=1, 0)
| table asset_name, serial, contract_status, contract_number, service_level, end_date, days_to_expiry, severity_bucket, business_owner, action_owner
| sort 0 - sev_n days_to_expiry
```

**Understanding this pipeline (contract posture, not power or alarms):** the base `search` is anchored on the real `cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation` label, with a defensive OR to `cisco:intersight:assetcontractinfo` in case a staging TA shortens the name—delete the extra branch if your `| stats count by sourcetype` does not show it. The `strptime` stack mirrors ISO8601 strings with and without a trailing `Z` and with fractional second noise. The `regdev_moid` `trim`/`replace` recipe is a best-effort normaliser for string versus lightly serialised object shapes; re-check against your `fieldsummary`. The two `join` blocks are true left joins: a contract with no `RegisteredDevice` still appears for renewal follow-up, which is a clue that claim or decommission work is out of date. The `case` for `service_tier` is intentionally short—expand regex lists as your offer catalog grows. The `where` on `LineStatus` filters quiet decommissions: use your own convention if the vendor ships additional lifecycle tokens. The numeric `sev_n` enforces a stable ordering independent of string collation.

**Alerting policy:** this is a procurement and vendor cadence, not a classic P1 on-call page. Save as a **weekly** or **twice weekly** report with a CSV or HTTP Event Collector action to your vendor-management queue. For leadership, add a `sendemail` or PDF renderer action monthly. Throttle to once per `contract_moid` per four weeks. Pair only with a bridge page for `severity_bucket=expired` on rows whose `change_ticket_required` from the lookup is `1` and whose `asset_role` is `production`—never the lab tier.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) In the Intersight user interface, open **Service Profiles** → the **Coverage & contracts** (wording can vary) panel for a known contract line and read **Contract** status, end date, and service level. Then drill **Operate → Servers →** pick a system **→ Inventory → Contract** and cross-check the same line items against the same wall-clock window, allowing the modular poll to refresh.
(2) In Splunk, run a narrow probe: `index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetcontractinformation" earliest=-7d@d | head 5` and read raw JSON. Confirm `EndDate` spellings, then re-run the full saved search. If a field is always null, sample five more devices—your TA may have aliased a parent path.
(3) Prove join keys: for one `regdev_moid` that appears in both `assetdeviceregistration` and `computephysicalsummary`, `| stats count` each side with `| search regdev_moid=<value>`. A systematic miss usually means the contract references a *registration* Moid, while `PhysicalSummary` used a *server* Moid; consult Cisco’s object relationship diagram for your form factor, then adjust the join to the documented key rather than duplicating the guess here.
(4) Compare to UC-19.1.20 and UC-19.1.21 only to explain sibling differences, not to prove the same data: `RunningFirmware` and **cond.HclStatus** are technical matrices; the contract end date is financial entitlement—your CIO reviews different slides.
(5) API parity: in a read-only tool, call `GET /api/v1/asset/ContractInformations?$top=1` and compare the JSON to `_raw` in Splunk for the same `Moid`. A consistent mismatch after adjusting for poll skew is a parser or TA version issue—open a Cisco ticket with both payloads.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
Layout (dashboard `Intersight — Commercial contract posture`):
• Single value: count `severity_bucket` = `expired`.
• Single value: count `critical_30d` with sparkline of prior eight weeks (saved as summary index for speed if needed).
• `table` panel: `sort 0` top fifty `days_to_expiry` ascending, columns exactly as the saved search, but hide internal MOIDs for executive tiles.
• Heatmap: `business_service` (lookup) on rows against `severity_bucket`.
**Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts:** wire the report on Sunday night local; route CSV to ServiceNow, Jira, or Coupa with your vendor queue project id; PDF to procurement leadership monthly.

RACI-style ownership: **Procurement / Vendor management** is action owner of renewal tasks; the compute platform team owns **asset_name** and serial truth; the security and compliance function owns audit attestation packets built from the same saved search. Use `intersight_contract_reader` in Splunk; map dashboard editors to a smaller `uc19_contracts_editor` role if you split duties.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
1. **No events** — the modular input is disabled, pointed at a wrong `account`, the API key expired, the forwarder cannot egress 443, or the MO filter list omits `asset.ContractInformation`. `splunkd.log` on the input host shows 401/403 and TLS proxy failures explicitly—fix the account scope before you tune SPL.
2. **Events but `ContractStatus` null** — upgrade or patch the TA, then `fieldsummary`. Some builds rename keys; extend `coalesce()` with the new spellings. Confirm JSON indexed extractions on the right sourcetype—never clone an unrelated `cisco:intersight` stanza that changes `KV_MODE` away from the vendor default without testing.
3. **`days_to_expiry` wild values** — `strptime` mismatch: capture `end_date_raw` into a `rex` to see hidden timezone offsets, add another `strptime` line for offset formats, and verify NTP. Remember **EndDate** is usually midnight UTC; procurement quarter close may be a local business calendar.
4. **Joins always empty** — `RegisteredDevice` format drift or multi-tenancy: compare string equality between feeds with `| eval chk=if(regdev_moid=parent_moid,1,0)` in a `join` test harness. A connector-only estate may lack `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` if you never enabled it—turn that input on, do not “fix” the issue by faking the sourcetype.
5. **Lookups not firing** — CSV header names must be exact (`service_tier` vs `Service tier`), UTF-8 without BOM, and the transforms belong to the same app the saved search uses; use **Settings → Lookups** to confirm sharing into the app context.
6. **Orphaned contracts** — a contract row whose `regdev_moid` never resolves usually means a decommissioned asset left in the commercial database; reconcile with CMDB, not a firewall change.
7. **Partner `Source` looks expired** — channel systems can lead Cisco by days on renewal sync—cross-check your partner’s portal, not a second RMA in Splunk.
8. **Rows disappear for some operators** — Intersight RBAC scopes that exclude `asset.ContractInformation` in one child account show an artificial empty set; validate with a full-admin key in a throwaway `index=_internal` `rest` test *never* in production, or ask Cisco to document the org scope the key can read. Also verify you did not apply an overly tight `srchFilter` to the new role. When all else fails, open a TAC case with: TA version, redacted `inputs.conf` stanza names, a single `ContractInformations` JSON payload, and the matching Splunk event.

Cross-reference: if you need technical lifecycle posture, use UC-19.1.20 (firmware) and UC-19.1.21 (HCL). If you need the live outage signal, use UC-19.1.19 (`cisco:intersight:alarms`). This UC-19.1.24 is the commercial contract and warranty window story for finance, vendor management, and audit cadence—a different audience and runbook, even though the same Intersight tenant backs all feeds. UC-19.1.27 remains the Nutanix AOS version axis, and UC-19.1.33 remains connector tunnel health, not entitlements.
grandmaExplanation: We look at the paperwork clock on your big shared computer systems—the dates that say when the vendor is still on the hook to help you fast if something physical breaks, and which service grade you are paying for. We line that up with who owns the budget for renewals and who owns the machines, so a surprise never lands the week a board or auditor asks whether you are still covered.
hardware: All Intersight-claimed endpoints that participate in an entitlement story you care to defend in front of finance: Cisco UCS B-series (B200 M5/M6/M7), C-series (C220/C240/C480 M5–M7), X-series (X9508 chassis with X210c M6/M7 plus X410c M6 blades), Fabric Interconnects (UCS 6324, 6332, 6454, 6536, 65108-class builds when Intersight lists them for your org), HyperFlex HX240c and HX220c families where contract objects ride on the same account, Intersight Workload Engine, and any UCS Director-connected nodes that appear as first-class Intersight assets in your subscription. The feed is about commercial coverage, not a complete listing of every serial that ever sat in a spare cage—if it is not in **asset.ContractInformation**, this UC is silent on purpose until claim and Cisco visibility catch up.
